Header file for the ECMA property lookup cache with functions to initialize, insert, lookup, and invalidate.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that defines the interface for a property lookup cache, part of the ECMA (European Computer Manufacturers Association) module. It provides function declarations for initializing the cache ([`ecma_lcache_init`](<#ecma_lcache_init>)), inserting entries into the cache ([`ecma_lcache_insert`](<#ecma_lcache_insert>)), looking up properties in the cache ([`ecma_lcache_lookup`](<#ecma_lcache_lookup>)), and invalidating cache entries ([`ecma_lcache_invalidate`](<#ecma_lcache_invalidate>)). These functions operate on objects of types `ecma_object_t`, `ecma_string_t`, and `ecma_property_t`, which are likely defined elsewhere in the ECMA module. The header file uses include guards to prevent multiple inclusions, ensuring that the declarations are only processed once during compilation.
# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### ecma\_lcache\_init<!-- {{#callable_declaration:ecma_lcache_init}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/base/ecma-lcache.h#L26>)

Initializes the property lookup cache.
- **Description**: Use this function to initialize the property lookup cache before performing any cache operations. This function must be called once before any other cache-related functions to ensure the cache is in a known state. If the cache is disabled via configuration, this function has no effect.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`ecma_lcache_init`](<ecma-lcache.c.md#ecma_lcache_init>)  (Implementation)


---
### ecma\_lcache\_insert<!-- {{#callable_declaration:ecma_lcache_insert}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/base/ecma-lcache.h#L27>)

Inserts a property into the property lookup cache.
- **Description**: Use this function to add a property to the lookup cache for a given object and property name. This function must be called with valid pointers for the object, property name, and property. The property must not already be cached, and it must be of type named data or named accessor. This function is typically used to optimize property access by caching frequently accessed properties.
- **Inputs**:
    - `object_p`: Pointer to the object that owns the property. Must not be null.
    - `prop_name_p`: Pointer to the property name. Must not be null.
    - `prop_p`: Pointer to the property. Must not be null and must not be already cached. The property type must be either named data or named accessor.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`ecma_lcache_insert`](<ecma-lcache.c.md#ecma_lcache_insert>)  (Implementation)


---
### ecma\_lcache\_invalidate<!-- {{#callable_declaration:ecma_lcache_invalidate}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/jerry-core/ecma/base/ecma-lcache.h#L29>)

Invalidates a property in the lookup cache.
- **Description**: Use this function to remove a specific property from the lookup cache of an object. This is necessary when the property is no longer valid or has been changed, ensuring that subsequent lookups do not return outdated information. The function must be called with valid pointers to an object, a property name, and a property that is currently cached. It is important to ensure that the property is of a type that can be cached, such as named data or named accessor properties.
- **Inputs**:
    - `object_p`: Pointer to the object containing the property. Must not be null. The caller retains ownership.
    - `prop_name_p`: Pointer to the property's name. Must not be null. The caller retains ownership.
    - `prop_p`: Pointer to the property to invalidate. Must not be null and must be a property that is currently cached. The caller retains ownership.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`ecma_lcache_invalidate`](<ecma-lcache.c.md#ecma_lcache_invalidate>)  (Implementation)
